title: GJR-GARCH Volatility Forecast for Dynamic Signal Gating
summary: The GJR-GARCH (Glosten-Jagannathan-Runkle GARCH) model extends standard GARCH by adding an asymmetry parameter that gives extra weight to negative return shocks — capturing the well-known 'leverage effect' where bad news causes larger volatility spikes than equivalent good news. Using the Python `arch` library (pure Python, no C dependencies unlike TA-Lib) with yfinance price history, Decifer can generate a 1-day-ahead conditional volatility forecast for SPY and each position. This forecast can gate signal scoring: if forecast_vol > 1.5x the 30-day average, reduce signal dimension weights or widen the entry threshold. QuantInsti benchmarks from 2025 confirm GJR-GARCH out-of-sample accuracy on SPY is measurably better than plain GARCH. The `arch` library is pip-installable with no system C libraries required.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — ML Feature Engineering & Predictive Signal Construction
expected_impact: +3–4% win rate via false signal suppression in vol spikes
